metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2598
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Impairment of neuronal branching and spine formation in the primary cultured hippocampal neurons in DGKB KO mice.
DGKB-induced branching and spine formation in the primary cultured hippocampal neurons.
Abnormality of synapse density and phosphatidic acid/diacylglycerol levels in the hippocampus of DGKB KO mice.

21/2/2018 Pim
Fig.5: "Impairment of spine formation in the primary cultured hippocampal neurons from DGKβ KO mice."
Fig.6: "DGKβ-induced branching and spine formation in the primary cultured hippocampal neurons from WT mice."
Fig.7: "Rescue of impaired branching and spine formation in the primary cultured hippocampal neurons from KO mice by DGKβ overexpression". Experimental description: Materials and Methods
Animals
Pregnant mice (day 14) (C57BL/6JJmsSlc) for the primary culture were obtained from Japan SLC Inc. (Hamamatsu, Japan). DGKβ KO mice were produced using the Sleeping Beauty transposon system.

Construction of plasmid encoding GFP-DGKβ and its splice variant form (C-cut mutant).
The cDNA encoding DGKβ was produced by a polymerase chain reaction (PCR) with cDNA for DGKβ as the template. The sense and anti-sense primers used were 5'-ATAGGATCCATGACAAACCAGGAAAAATGG-3' and 5'-ATAGGATCCTCATTCCTTGCTTCGGTTT-3'.
